text: The laparoscopic exploration is conducted in reverse T-position. Three 8mm and one 12mm robotic trocars, along with one assistant trocar, are inserted. The jejunal diverticular segment, located 20 centimeters distal to the right, is marked with a silk suture.
The surgeon observes multiple diverticula, each measuring up to three or four centimeters, distributed along an approximately one hundred centimeter segment of the jejunum.
The surgeon marks the distal end of the jejunal segment to be resected with a silk suture. The meso-oxygen is released using a vessel sealer, progressing from proximal to distal along the segment.
The surgeon uses a robotic stopper to transect the distal end of the intestine, which is confirmed to have normal circulation with ICG imaging.
The surgeon uses a robotic stapler to transect the proximal end of the jejunal segment after confirming control with ICG. Approximately a one hundred centimeter segment of the jejunum is resected.
The surgeon removes the resected intestinal segment through a suprapubic incision. The intestinal ends are then closed with sutures, ensuring a secure closure of the resection site.
The surgeon performs cetabular incisions on the intestinal segment, preparing for further surgical manipulation.
The surgeon completes a side-to-side anastomosis using a robotic stapler. The cetabular incisions are then sutured using a velar suture technique.
